content:
# Joshua Chapter 18 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 241-242 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_018_page_001.jpg`

1. And all the congregation of the sons of Yisrael assembled [at] Shiloh, and they caused the tent of meeting to dwell there; and the land was subdued before them.

2. And there remained among the sons of Yisrael who had not received their inheritance, seven tribes.

3. And Yehoshua said to the sons of Yisrael, "Until when [will] you [be] slack to go in to possess the land which YHWH, God of your fathers, has given to you?

4. Provide for yourselves three men for [each] tribe, and I will send them, and they shall arise and walk about in the land, and they shall write it according to their inheritance, and they shall come to me.

5. And they shall divide it into seven portions. Yehudah shall stand upon his border from the south, and the house of Yosef shall stand upon their border from the north.

6. And you shall write the land [in] seven portions and bring [it] to me here, and I will cast for you [a] lot here before YHWH our God.

7. For there is no portion for the Levites in your midst, for the priesthood of YHWH [is] their inheritance. And Gad and Reuven and the half-tribe of Menasheh have received their inheritance on the other side of the Yarden eastward, which Moshe, servant of YHWH, gave to them."

8. And the men arose and went. And Yehoshua commanded the ones going to write the land, saying, "Go and walk about in the land and write it and return to me, and here I will cast for you [a] lot before YHWH in Shiloh."

9. And the men went and passed through the land and wrote it by cities [in] seven portions upon a scroll, and they came to Yehoshua, to the camp, [at] Shiloh.

10. And Yehoshua cast for them [a] lot in Shiloh before YHWH, and Yehoshua divided there the land to the sons of Yisrael according to their divisions.

11. And the lot of the tribe of the sons of Binyamin came up according to their clans, and the border of their lot went out between the sons of Yehudah and between the sons of Yosef.

12. And their border was on the north side from the Yarden, and the border went up to the side of Yericho from the north, and it went up in the hill country westward, and its goings out were [at] the wilderness of Beit Aven.

13. And the border passed from there [toward] Luz, to the side of Luz — it [is] Beit El — southward, and the border went down [to] Atarot Addar, upon the mountain which [is] from the south of lower Beit Choron.

14. And the border was drawn and turned on the west side southward, from the mountain which [is] before Beit Choron southward, and its goings out were to Qiryat Baal — it [is] Qiryat Yearim — a city of the sons of Yehudah. This [was] the west side.

15. And the south side [was] from the edge of Qiryat Yearim, and the border went out westward, and it went out to the spring of the waters of Nephtoach.

16. And the border went down to the edge of the mountain which [is] before the valley of Ben Hinnom, which [is] in the valley of the Rephaim northward, and it went down [to the] valley of Hinnom, to the side of the Yevusite southward, and it went down [to] Ein Rogel.

17. And it was drawn from the north and went out [to] Ein Shemesh, and it went out to Gelilot, which [is] opposite the ascent of Adummim, and it went down [to the] stone of Bohan son of Reuven.

18. And it passed to the side opposite the Aravah northward, and it went down [to] the Aravah.

19. And the border passed to the side of Beit Choglah northward, and the goings out of the border were to the tongue of the Sea of the Salt northward, to the south end of the Yarden. This [was] the southern border.

20. And the Yarden bordered it on the east side. This [was] the inheritance of the sons of Binyamin, by its borders all around, according to their clans.

21. And the cities of the tribe of the sons of Binyamin according to their clans were: Yericho, and Beit Choglah, and Emeq Qetzitz,

22. and Beit ha-Aravah, and Tzemarayim, and Beit El,

23. and ha-Avvim, and ha-Parah, and Ophrah,

24. and Kephar ha-Ammonai, and ha-Ophni, and Gava — twelve cities and their villages.

25. Giveon, and ha-Ramah, and Beerot,

26. and ha-Mitzpeh, and ha-Kephirah, and ha-Motzah,

27. and Reqem, and Yirpeel, and Taralah,

28. and Tzela, ha-Eleph, and ha-Yevusi — it [is] Yerushalayim — Givat, Qiryat — fourteen cities and their villages. This [was] the inheritance of the sons of Binyamin according to their clans.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v1 — "caused to dwell" (וַיַּשְׁכִּנוּ):** The Hiphil of שׁכן is rendered literally; the Tabernacle was actively "caused to dwell" at Shiloh, preserving the causative sense of the Hebrew stem.

2. **v3 — "slack" (מִתְרַפִּים):** The Hitpael participle of רפה, meaning "showing yourselves slack/lax." Yehoshua rebukes the remaining tribes for their delay.

3. **v5 — "stand upon" (יַעֲמֹד עַל):** Literally "shall stand upon his border," meaning each tribe will remain within its already-assigned territory while the remaining land is surveyed.

4. **v12 — "Beit Aven" (בֵּית אָוֶן):** Literally "House of Wickedness." Retained in transliterated form as a proper place name in the Codex.

5. **v13 — "Luz, it is Beit El":** The Codex preserves the older Canaanite name Luz alongside the Israelite name Beit El, attesting to the renaming tradition of Genesis 28:19.

6. **v14 — "Qiryat Baal, it is Qiryat Yearim":** The older Canaanite theophoric name (invoking Baal) is preserved, with the Israelite renaming noted. Qiryat Yearim means "City of Forests."

7. **v16 — "valley of Ben Hinnom" (גֵּי בֶן הִנֹּם):** Literally "valley of the son of Hinnom," the geographic name that later gives rise to Gehinnom/Gehenna.

8. **v17 — "Gelilot" (גְּלִילוֹת):** Meaning "circuits" or "regions." In the parallel passage (Joshua 15:7) the name Gilgal appears; both derive from the root גלל ("to roll").

9. **v19 — "tongue of the Sea of the Salt":** The Hebrew לְשֹׁן ("tongue") describes the northern bay or inlet of the Dead Sea, a characteristic geographic metaphor in boundary descriptions.

10. **v28 — City count discrepancy:** The text lists the second group as "fourteen cities," though counting the names yields thirteen. Ancient scribal traditions vary on whether Tzela and ha-Eleph are one city (Tzela-ha-Eleph) or two. The Leningrad Codex punctuation may support reading them as a single compound name, which would resolve the count if Givat and Qiryat are similarly compound (Givat-Qiryat = Givat Shaul). This is a well-known textual question in the Masoretic tradition.
